perturbation.original_text: For the purpose of this Agreement, Affiliates means with respect to a party, any corporation or entity which (i) controls either directly or indi rectly that party, or (ii) is c ontrolled directly or indirectly by that party, or (iii) is under common control with the party.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose of this Agreement, Affiliates means with respect to a party, any corporation or entity which (i) controls either directly or indi rectly that party, or (ii) is c ontrolled directly or indirectly by that party, or (iii) is under common control with the party. However, for obligations relating to Section 5, 'Affiliates' will only include entities with over 75% direct ownership by the party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction. The definition of 'Affiliates' is changed specifically for Section 5, creating confusion about which definition should be used in that section. Now, Section 5 refers to a subset of the initially defined affiliates based on 75% direct ownership, causing in-text contradiction as the original definition is broad.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a general definition of 'Affiliates'. The changed text modifies this definition specifically for Section 5, limiting it to entities with over 75% direct ownership. The contradicted text outlines confidentiality obligations for the 'Receiving Party' regarding disclosure of Confidential Information to its employees, agents, and subcontractors. Since the definition of 'Affiliates' has been narrowed for Section 5, it introduces a contradiction. The Receiving Party's obligations under the contradicted text might apply to a different group of 'Affiliates' than what is defined in Section 5 after the change, thus creating an inconsistency regarding to whom the Receiving Party's obligations extend in Section 5, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidential and restricted property of the Disclosing Party. However, any data relating to marketing strategies will not be considered Confidential Information regardless of the marking.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Confidential Information'. The change adds an exception that marketing strategies data will not be considered Confidential Information, causing an in-text contradiction with the initial definition. This is in-text contradiction with sections that refer to the entire 'Confidential Information' without this new limitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property. 4.1 The Receiving Party for purposes of confidentiality is bound by its managers, emplo yees, agents, for any purpose, and principals. 4.2 The Receiving Party acknowledges that the technical specifications as well as all documents to be disclosed are not subject to appropriation, remain the property of Disclosing Party or any third party, as applicable. 4.3 Each Party agrees to use Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement. N o license or right under any intel lectual property right is grant ed by the mere transmittal of Confidential Information to the Receiving Party , nor shall such a transmission c onstitute any r epresentation o r warranty by the Disclosing Party with respect to infringement of intellectual property rights o f third parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information' by stating that marketing strategies data will not be considered Confidential Information regardless of the marking. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Receiving Party must keep confidential 'all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party'. The addition in the changed text creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, making it unclear whether marketing strategies data is subject to the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted text, a definition that previously had no exception.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Information" shall include all written information , verbal or otherwise presented in tangible or intangible way, including, but n ot limited to, trade secrets, d iscoveries, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, technology, products, designs, specifications, drawings, diagra ms, data, computer programs, business activities and operations, repo rts, studies and other technical , commercial, financial in formation of each Party or its Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Information" shall include all written information , verbal or otherwise presented in tangible or intangible way, including, but n ot limited to, trade secrets, d iscoveries, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, technology, products, designs, specifications, drawings, diagra ms, data, computer programs, business activities and operations, repo rts, studies and other technical , commercial, financial in formation of each Party or its Affiliates. However, source code is explicitly excluded from the definition of 'Information'.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds an exclusion for 'source code' from the definition of 'Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction, since the broader definition initially encompasses everything, now source code is not information. The in-text contradiction arises in sections that require confidentiality, use and return of all information, as Source Code is not part of it anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information (either Confidential Information or not) fur nished under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be returned to it or destroyed promptly at its reque st (together with all relevant copies, extracts, plan s, schematics or other reproduct ions). ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS.” NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE OF THE CONF IDENTIAL INFORMATION, AND EACH PARTY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY, NON-INFRINGEMENT OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'source code' from the definition of 'Information', whereas the original text included all information without exception. The contradicted text requires the return of 'all information', and the exclusion of source code creates an inconsistency as source code is not part of it anymore. This creates a direct contradiction since the definition of information is changed such that it conflicts with the requirement to return all information. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property. Unless the Receiving Party is compelled by law, in that case it is not required to keep it confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates confidentiality. The change adds an exception if the Receiving Party is compelled by law, causing a contradiction. The legal exception now in-text contradict with other clauses requiring confidentiality, leading to uncertainty about what takes precedence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation, stating that the Receiving Party is not required to keep Confidential Information confidential if compelled by law. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that the Receiving Party restrict disclosure, ensure its agents abide by non-disclosure terms, maintain confidentiality, and notify the other party of any misappropriation. The introduction of a legal exception directly conflicts with the absolute confidentiality requirements specified in the contradicted text, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information . However, the Receiving Party is only required to make reasonable effort to protect the data.
perturbation.explanation: The change qualifies the requirement to maintain confidentiality to 'reasonable effort', introducing in-text contradiction with the original more strict obligation to 'maintain the confidentiality and take proper measures'. This creates ambiguity and uncertaintity on the degree to which confidentiality should be maintained. The in-text contradiction is related to the meaning of the initial term of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that the breach of secrecy has the c haracter of serious irregularity and can cause serious damages to the other Party. 9.1 In case of breach of any obligation set forth herein by th e Receiving Party , the Disclosing Party shall recover any and all damages. Notwithstanding any other provisio n herein, neither Party shall be liable for indirect damages o r loss of profits. 9.2 Accordingly, the actual or t hreatened unauthorized disclosu re or use of any Information shall give the Disclosing Party the right to seek injunctive relief restraining such unauthoriz ed disclosure or use, without the necessity of proving actual damages, in addition to any other remedy otherwise available to the Disclosing Party . Any third party owner of Information disclosed by a party he reto shall be an intended third party beneficiary of this Agreement and shall be entitled to rely upon and directly enforce the terms and conditions hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "However, the Receiving Party is only required to make reasonable effort to protect the data." to the original text. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that breach of secrecy has serious consequences and the Disclosing Party shall recover any and all damages in case of breach. The change weakens the Receiving Party's obligation, creating ambiguity and conflicting with the Disclosing Party's right to recover damages, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be in force on the Execution Date and shall remain in force for a period of twenty-four (24) months. Notwithstanding the term of the Agreement , the Parties undertake to maintain the confidentiality obligation for a period of three (3) years after Confidential Information disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be in force on the Execution Date and shall remain in force for a period of twenty-four (24) months. Notwithstanding the term of the Agreement , the Parties undertake to maintain the confidentiality obligation for a period of three (3) years after Confidential Information disclosure. However, for marketing-related confidential information, the obligation extends only to 1 year.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. While generally it's three years post-disclosure, for marketing-related confidential information, it's reduced to one year, which contradicts the earlier statement of a general three-year term. Creates uncertainty with the duration of each confidentiality, considering the nature of the confidential information
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property. 4.1 The Receiving Party for purposes of confidentiality is bound by its managers, emplo yees, agents, for any purpose, and principals. 4.2 The Receiving Party acknowledges that the technical specifications as well as all documents to be disclosed are not subject to appropriation, remain the property of Disclosing Party or any third party, as applicable. 4.3 Each Party agrees to use Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement. N o license or right under any intel lectual property right is grant ed by the mere transmittal of Confidential Information to the Receiving Party , nor shall such a transmission c onstitute any r epresentation o r warranty by the Disclosing Party with respect to infringement of intellectual property rights o f third parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an inconsistency regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. The original text states a general three-year confidentiality term, while the changed text introduces a one-year exception for marketing-related confidential information. This contradicts the general three-year term implicitly stated within the contradicted text, creating uncertainty about the applicable confidentiality period and therefore generating a strong in-text contradiction, as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
